segment-text: EXT/INT. JEEP - WEST COAST OF JUTLAND - AFTERNOON
World War 2 is over.
CU A Danish soldier, Carl (41)(military rank: Sergeant) is

driving along in a jeep. He turns into a gravel road. After a while he comes to the dunes overlooking the beach. Carl parks the jeep and steps out. He walks over the dunes. On his back is a big backpack from which little black flags are sticking out. Carl stops on top of one of the dunes and looks across the beach. Clearly, this is a post-war landscape. As far the eye can see the beach is covered with lines of barbwire and signs saying. 'DANGER! MINES.' Carl pushes some sand off with his foot. (He looks at a mine map) A wooden plank (Pansermine) appears beneath the sand. Carl puts his backpack down and digs further up ahead. Another plank appears. Carl puts his backpack back on and walks along the planks down towards the sea. He treads carefully, it is obvious that the entire beach is littered with mines. You can even see some of them poke up through the sand.
INT. ARMY TRUCK - AFTERNOON

Carl goes all the way to the water before he stops. He looks down the beach. He pulls a flag out of his backpack and sticks it into the sand. Then we walks along the water. CU of young German soldier on the back of an army truck. This is Sebastian(16). Besides Sebastian are 11 other German boys.

the others, they are holding hands. Sebastian peers out through the a gap in tarpaulin cloth that covers the back of the truck. He squints as the setting sun gets in his eyes. The truck is driving along a straight road through a forest near the Danish North Sea coastline.
EXT. COUNTRY ROAD - LATE AFTERNOON

Carl is driving his jeep along. He drives past long lines of German prisoners of war walking south along the road, heading back down towards Germany. Further ahead there is a scuffle between some Danes and the German POV's. Carl pulls up to them. Honks the horn. He stops and gets out. Carl drags the Danes away. The Germans are about to continue walking. Carl turns towards them.
The Germans wait.
You stay here. Understood?

Carl goes over and talks to the Danes. (From afar) We can't hear what they are saying. The Danes point over at the Germans. Carl turns around and goes over to the Germans. He smiles at them. He stands there for a bit. He offers one of them a cigarette. The German takes one and smiles back. Carl takes out a lighter. Long beat. Carl headbutts the German. He goes down. Carl looks at the other Germans. They make no move to interfere. They look scared. Nobody speaks.
EXT. ARMY BARRACKS SONDERBORG AREA - LATE AFTERNOON

The German who was knocked down by Carl gets back up. They start walking. The truck with Sebastian and the boys drives through a gate. It stops on a large open square. We see several trucks pull up next to it. Pebbles crunch under the heavy truck tires. German prisoners of war in all ages spill out from the trucks and settle in the square. A few Danish armed soldiers are there to receive them.
EXT. ARMY BARRACKS SONDERBORG AREA - LATE AFTERNOON
About 70 German prisoners of war of various ages are lined up outside of the square outside the main building. Quite a few of them are teenagers. They look wretched in their worn-out torn uniforms.
out of the main building. Ebbe walks briskly and stops in front of the Germans. Ebbe smiles and addresses the POW's in German.

To those of you who are still unaware of what you are doing here: You have been brought here to Denmark to clean up after the war. My name is Lieutenant Ebbe Jensen from the Danish Pioneer Corps. (Minenkommando Danemark) Denmark is neither your friend, nor your enemy. Having said that, do not expect the Danes to welcome you with open arms. Nobody wants you Germans here. For us you serve one purpose only: To clear the Danish west coast of German land mines that you put there in the first place. I know that most of you have never done any minesweeping or de-mining. That is why we have brought you here, before you are to de-mine the beaches. We will teach you as much as we can in the little time we have. In other words. We are here to help. EBBE (in German) (CONT'D) Welcome to Denmark!
INT. SLEEPING QUARTERS/ BARRACKS - EVENING

Sebastian and the other boys walk in a line down a long hallway. They are shown into the sleeping quarters. A small room. Filled with bunk beds without mattresses. Sebastian looks around. occupied. It seems most of the beds are Sebastian spots an empty bed, next to the window. sits down on the bed. One of the older boys, Helmut(19), comes over. at Sebastian. He looks He stands there for a long time before he speaks.
Sebastian looks at the boy. Clearly, this is not his bed. Sebastian thinks about standing his ground. Decides against it. He gets up. Of course.
Sebastian finds a spot on the floor, where he sits down. He looks around the room. He pulls his blanket around him.
INT. INTERIM CLASSROOM - MORNING

Looks around at the others before Sebastian lies down. he shuts his eyes. There is not enough beds for everyone and several others have also had to settle for the floor. Sebastian and most of the boys he came with in the truck are standing to attention in a small classroom. The young commanding officer Ebbe comes in. He is flanked by two Danish soldiers. He stands in front of the boys. On a table are several land mines. He pulls down a map of Ebbe looks around the room. Denmark.
Ebbe smiles. Ebbe outlines the western coast of Denmark on the map with his cane.
He looks at the boys.

My only purpose here is to teach you lot how to find and disarm these mines. Nothing else. This is what I do. This is what I am good at. In other words, I don't need to know your names, your background. I don't need to get to know you. You have each other for that. I am simply not interested.
Nobody says anything.
Most of them raise a hand.
How many have disarmed one?
Only a few hands. He picks up a German teller Ebbe walks over to the table.

INT. SLEEPING QUARTERS/ BARRACKS - NIGHT

Sebastian and the boys are in their beds. Most have gone to sleep. Sebastian looks around. Ernst (one of the twin brothers) is having a nightmare. Werner(17) his brother carefully wakes Ernst up. He whispers to him. Holds him. Ernst calms down. Werner keeps watch over his brother. Sebastian closes his eyes.
EXT. ARMY BARRACKS - DAY
Following day. Sebastian and some of the other boys are crawling around in a giant sand box. Ebbe, flanked by two soldiers, is walking among the boys. Ebbe speaks while observing the boys.
A bit further away. Carl is over by a barn. He is loading wooden planks onto a trailer. Peter (39), a Danish soldier is helping him. While working Carl looks over at Ebbe. Peter looks at Carl.
I thought you two were inseparable?
He keeps loading the trailer. Carl doesn't answer. Peter stops.
In my opinion you should put it behind you, Carl. The war has ruined too God damn much already, right?
Did anyone ask for your opinion?
No, but I am just saying, that he did everything he could to help you from over there.
Carl looks at Peter. He goes over to him. Carl puts his face right up against Peter's. This makes Peter nervous. Carl smiles at him.
Did he? And what do you know about it, little Peter? How did he help me, exactly? Or my dad for that matter? None of you did shit. You all sneaked out and ran. You call that help?
Peter says nothing.
3 years we were locked up. you. Not him!
Carl gently slaps Peter on the side of the head.
And what did you do, exactly, over there in Sweden? Tell me.
We were mobilizing..
Mobilizing ... More like hiding out and fucking all the Swedish women and having a good time.
And then you return all hero-like.. After the danger is gone, promoted with pretty new ranks?
What are you now? Senior Sergeant? How the fuck did that happen?
Peter says nothing.
Is there anything else you want to say to me?
Peter shakes his head.
No? I didn't think so. So keep your mouth shut.
Ebbe is walking in between the boys.
What do you think that means for you guys?
No one answers.
Carl looks at Peter's uniform.
Ebbe holds up his little wooden stick.
Unlike me, the stick is now your new best friend. Suddenly Ebbe whacks one of the boys Ludvig (16) across the hands with the stick. Boom!
You just lost your hands.
Keep going. Go on. There is no time to feel sorry for oneself.
Ebbe is standing over him.
Ludvig fiddles again. Ludvig fiddles with the mine once more. Ebbe whacks him again. His hands are red from the blows.
Boom! I believe we have our first terminal casualty here. You don't want to see your family again?
Are you really going to be the first one to die?
Ludvig mumbles.
No, what?
No, lieutenant! I am not to be the first one to die! I am happy to hear it!
Ludvig's hand are shaking.
He tries again.
Sebastian and the other boys are taking note of what is happening, while they are prodding away in the sand. Manfred (16) is digging next to Sebastian.
Idiot.
Ebbe hears it. He comes over to Sebastian and Manfred.
Is there anything you want to share with the group?
No, sir!
Ebbe addresses Manfred. Ebbe looks over at Sebastian.
No, sir. I have nothing I want to share with the group. No really. I saw you smile. Do tell. Lord knows, your friends here could do with something to smile about.
It was nothing. Are you sure?
Yes, sir, lieutenant. Absolutely sure.
Most of the boys look down again and keep searching for mines.

Sebastian prods with the stick, carefully. Sebastian hits something. He carefully digs with his hands. A teller mine (a practice mine) appears. He quickly disarms it. He picks it up and puts it away. see that this is his fifth one.

I didn't think the lieutenant was interested in knowing our names, sir?
Ebbe whacks Sebastian's fingers with his cane. pulls his hands back.
Ebbe walks on.
Remember that your stick is your best friend now. Your own little magic wand!
Ebbe stops behind one of the others boys - Helmut(19)(the
boy who took Sebastian's bed). Ebbe whacks Helmut's hands.

Get moving!

Right in his face. Helmut is right in front of Sebastian. Sebastian doesn't answer. In a calm voice he says.

EXT. GRAVEL ROAD / WEST COAST - DAY

The army truck stops. This is the end of the line for them. The boys jump off the truck which drives off. The boys are assembled in the quietness. They look around. They are outside a barbwire fence. A sign says: Achtung Minen! Danger Mines! No Access! In front go the entrance are two guards armed with guns and rifles. Sebastian and the boys pass the guard point. They head down the the small gravel road. Towards the dunes and the sea.
EXT. GRAVEL ROAD / WEST COAST - DAY

The boys march along a gravel road with barb-wire on either side. The boys and Sebastian reach an old tarred wooden fishing hut inside the fenced off area. The hut is right in the middle of the dunes. It is a beautiful setting. If it hadn't for all the barbwire and the fencing it would have been scenic. Not far from the fishing hut is a little run-down twowinged farmhouse. We see, that a girl (5) is playing just outside the farmhouse.
EXT. FISHING HUT - DAY

He looks casually at the boys before he jumps out and walks over to the farmhouse. Carl knocks on the door to the farmhouse.

A tiny room with a low ceiling. Carl goes in first. The room has a simple bed with a mattress, a small table and a chair. His expression says it all. He is clearly not happy with the standard of the accommodation.
I know it is not much, but ...

They will keep to the hut.

Okay then ... if you need anything or have any questions, let me know.
Okay.

EXT. DUNES - AFTERNOON

A little later. Carl is walking through the dunes. boys walk after him in single file. They reach the top of the dunes. Carl stops. He looks out across the sea and down to the beach. Despite the bunkers and all the barbwire spread out along the beach, it is a stunning sight for Carl and the boys. Waves crash onto the beach.
EXT. BUNKER - DAY
Carl walks down the dunes and onto the beach itself. The boys follow. Single file. He climbs onto a bunker, 6 meters up. Carl is standing on top of the bunker looking down.
Come up here.

The boys climb up. Carl is balanced on the very edge of the bunker. He leans into the wind. The wind is strong enough to hold him at an angle without him falling over. Carl looks back at the boys. He smiles.
Can you see the black flag down there?
Carl points to the north along the beach. The boys look for the flag. They see it. It is a long way away. At least 500 meters.
Carl turns around and looks south. Down the beach is another flag. He points again.
In the area between those flags and the road are 22.000 mines.
You will disarm all of them. You do this, and you get to go home. Do you understand? Then you can go home. But not a second before. (MORE)
With six mines an hour, and provided you don't blow yourselves up, it will take you three months. Yes, sir.
The boys grimace despairingly. Carl turns and look at Sebastian. Sebastian looks at him skeptically. Carl goes over to him.
Are you sure?
Carl looks at the boys.
Absolutely sure, sir!
No, sir.
Do you want to say something?

The boys are ready with long sticks and long yarns of string. Carl is crouching on the sand. He scoops away some sand to reveal a wooden beam just below the sand.
Can everyone see these? Yes, sir.

These rafters run from the dunes down towards the sea approximately every 150 meters. You can step safely up to 1 meter on either side of such a rafter. Some are almost impossible to see, some have rotted away. You must find every single one of these rafters in the area, before we begin. It is absolutely essential that we clear the beach systematically in a precise grid.
You start by clearing out a safe path here. We need a passage from here and all the way up to the dunes. Is that understood? Yes, sir. Beat. You.
Yes, you with the map. Come here.
